dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}. Forgive me, I don't normally bother travelers with my troubles. But fate has left me no other door to knock on.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The blight came without warning. One morning the crops were green and thriving, the next they were blackened and rotting in the soil. Every field in {location} was hit: weeks of growth destroyed overnight. {target_npc} says it's a soil fungus, something that's been dormant in the ground for years and chose now to wake up. The only remedy is a compound made from {quest_item}, but it only grows in the deep wilds {location_hint}, where the {enemy_type} have been hunting anything that moves.
dialogueChunks.outro: We did everything right, {player_name}. Planted on time, tended carefully, prayed for rain. And the earth itself betrayed us.

playerResponses.text: Nature can be cruel, but it can be fought. I'll find what you need.
A blight with only one cure? Sounds expensive. What can you offer?
Soil fungus is beyond my expertise. You need an alchemist, not an adventurer.

dialogueChunks.intro: I've been staring at this cup for an hour, {player_name}. Can't bring myself to drink it because I keep thinking: what if this is the last clean water we have?
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The mine that sustained {location} for three generations collapsed last week. Not from an earthquake, not from sabotage: just age. The timbers rotted through and the whole thing came down. Twelve families depended on that mine for their livelihood, and now they have nothing. {target_npc} found traces of a new vein {location_hint}, but the area is thick with {enemy_type} and we'd need {quest_item} to shore up any new excavation. Bad luck, piled on bad luck, piled on worse.
dialogueChunks.outro: Some people would say we're cursed. I'd say we're just unlucky enough that it feels the same.

playerResponses.text: Luck changes. Let me help change yours.
I'll scout the new vein and clear the way. But luck isn't free.
Collapsed mines and dangerous territory? That's a dangerous combination I'd rather avoid.

dialogueChunks.intro: I should warn you, {player_name}: what I'm about to tell you is the kind of story that makes people walk away. I hope you're different.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A sickness has been creeping through {location} for weeks. Slow, insidious: starts with a cough and ends with people too weak to lift a spoon. {target_npc} was the first to fall ill and hasn't left their bed in ten days. The alchemist says the only treatment requires {quest_item} found in the marshes {location_hint}, but every forager we've sent has come back empty-handed or not come back at all. The {enemy_type} have claimed the marshes, and they don't share.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're not being attacked. We're not at war. We're just slowly, quietly dying. And nobody seems to care.

playerResponses.text: I care. Show me where these marshes are, and I'll bring back what the alchemist needs.
Marshes crawling with {enemy_type}? I'll go, but it won't be pleasant.
A slow plague in a swamp full of monsters? I value my health too much.

dialogueChunks.intro: You know what the cruelest part is, {player_name}? We were doing well. For the first time in years, {location} was thriving.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Then the trade road collapsed. A whole section of cliff gave way and buried the only route connecting us to the outside world. Overnight, our exports became worthless and our imports vanished. Merchants who used to come weekly stopped coming entirely. {target_npc} tried to blaze a new trail {location_hint}, but the {enemy_type} have infested every alternative path. Without {quest_item} from the outside, the craftsmen can't work, the alchemist can't brew, and the settlement that was thriving a month ago is slowly starving.
dialogueChunks.outro: We didn't make an enemy. We didn't provoke anyone. The cliff just... fell. And now we're dying of isolation.

playerResponses.text: Isolation can be broken. I'll open a way through.
A settlement cut off from trade is a settlement in trouble. I'll help, but at a cost.
Reopening a trade route through monster territory is more than a single job.
